response: Originating from 1960s U.S. defense research and the 1969 launch of ARPANET, the internet evolved through foundational milestones like the 1983 adoption of TCP/IP and Tim Berners-Lee’s 1989 invention of the World Wide Web. Commercialization and public accessibility accelerated through the 1990s and 2000s, driven by user-friendly browsers, the dot-com boom, social media platforms, and the 2007 smartphone revolution. Today, this decades-long technological progression has created a ubiquitous global infrastructure connecting over 5 billion people and fundamentally underpinning modern commerce, communication, and daily life.
